data.current_description:
Get the messages related to a sent message identified by `id`.

For more information how this work, see the `relatedSentMessageId` field in the message.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve messages related to a sent message
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all received messages that are related to a specific sent message identified by the `id` parameter. This operation is useful for tracking message conversations and replies. The relationship between messages is established through the `relatedSentMessageId` field. Returns an array of message objects containing the related received messages.
data.reasoning: The current description is vague about what 'related' means and lacks clarity on the return value. The suggestion clarifies the relationship mechanism, explains the use case, and specifies the response format for better AI agent understanding.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a paginated list of phone numbers that are blocked on your account. Blocked numbers are phone numbers to which your account is not permitted to send messages. These can be created via the API, by recipients replying with STOP messages, or by BulkSMS administrators. Returns a paginated collection of blocked number entries with their creation dates.

message: Operation 'post_/credit/transfer' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks details about required input (TransferEntry schema), expected response format, and side effects (immediate deduction/addition of credits). Added specifics about what the request body should contain, what is returned, and the immediate nature of the credit transfer for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
Before you can use the transfer credits endpoint, the _credit-transfer facility_ must be activated for your account.  You can request activation from `support@bulksms.com`. 

The recipient must be an enabled account that uses the same currency as your account.
data.description_suggestion: Transfer SMS credits from your account to another enabled BulkSMS account. The credit-transfer facility must be activated on your account (request from support@bulksms.com). The recipient account must be enabled and use the same currency as your account. Requires a TransferEntry request body specifying the recipient account and credit amount. Returns confirmation of the transfer with transaction details. Credits are immediately deducted from your account and added to the recipient's account.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about required input (TransferEntry schema), expected response format, and side effects (immediate deduction/addition of credits). Added specifics about what the request body should contain, what is returned, and the immediate nature of the credit transfer for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'get_/messages/send' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary is vague ('by simple GET or POST' is not action-focused). Description lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't emphasize that this is a legacy/simple interface. Improved summary to focus on the action (sending a single message) and added output details about the response format and clearer positioning as a simplified/legacy option.

data.current_summary: Send message by simple GET or POST
data.current_description:
A really simple interface for people who require a GET mechanism to submit a single message.

The URI is interpreted as UTF-8. HTTP Basic Auth is used for authentication.

__Note__ BulkSMS recommends that you use the more flexible Send Messages Operation when submitting SMS messages from your application.

Here is an example of a GET
```http
GET /v1/messages/send?to=%2b270000000&body=Hello%20World
```

You can also use the same parameters to POST form encoded fields to `/messages`.
Here is an example of a POST
```http
POST /v1/messages
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

to=%2b27000000000&body=Hello+World
```
data.summary_suggestion: Send single message via GET or POST
data.description_suggestion: A simplified interface for submitting a single SMS message using GET or POST with query/form parameters. Supports UTF-8 encoding and HTTP Basic Auth authentication. Returns a message submission response with status and message ID. Note: BulkSMS recommends using the more flexible POST /messages endpoint for production applications. This endpoint is provided for simple use cases and legacy integrations.
data.reasoning: Summary is vague ('by simple GET or POST' is not action-focused). Description lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't emphasize that this is a legacy/simple interface. Improved summary to focus on the action (sending a single message) and added output details about the response format and clearer positioning as a simplified/legacy option.
